sprint: Platform Hardening — Draft Asset Architecture, Phase E: verification, cross-owner resolution fix, and canonical doc
task: Closes the full Draft Asset Architecture plan (Phases A-E). Full end-to-end regression across both surfaces (author->save->reload->Build/Publish->Studio-render), a genuinely forced network-failure recovery test, and one real, previously-undiscovered gap found while verifying Magic Card recall specifically, fixed at its root: AssetStore.resolve() always computed the Storage object path from the CURRENT session's own owner id, with no way to resolve an asset belonging to a DIFFERENT owner -- exactly the recall case, where js/magicCard.js's _pullRecalledProjects adopts another device's Creator project as a brand-new local copy but any vihu-asset: reference it still carries was authored under the ORIGINAL device's own session. Fix: resolve(ref, opts) gained an optional opts.ownerId -- a FALLBACK, tried only after the current session's own owner id fails first, never before it -- threaded through js/magicCard.js's recallOwnerId stamp, js/projectManager.js's serialize()/deserialize() (carried on AppState.project AND stamped per-slide, since renderer/slideRenderer.js takes no direct AppState dependency), renderer/slideRenderer.js's _ensureDecorationImage, js/objectStrip.js's _renderThumb, and js/pictureStudio.js's open()/js/contextPanel.js's _cropRotateArtwork. A brand-new local upload made after a recall still resolves via the fast current-session-first path and never risks misrouting. Verified via a new 30-assertion Playwright suite (real Build byte-identical to the uploaded fixture; a real reload+Publish-Studio-render proof of Rule 5 fidelity for a vihu-asset: reference; a genuinely forced offline-then-recovered upload for both surfaces with the real 5s backoff honored; the cross-owner fix proven four ways including the full real _pullRecalledProjects->deserialize() chain) plus full regression across every prior Phase A/B/C/D suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all unchanged. New canonical docs/DRAFT_ASSET_ARCHITECTURE.md mirrors docs/THEME_REPOSITORY_ARCHITECTURE.md's own shape. No cache-bust bump needed for root index.html (no ?v= convention on its script tags); tools/world-builder-v2/index.html's own ?v= was bumped since its shared js/assetStore.js load changed. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Creator Governing Rules change.
